pr.title: Clean up `dolphin/types.h` and change empty brace formatting
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Moved the project’s common platform typedefs and constants out of `include/dolphin/types.h` into `include/Runtime/platform.h`, leaving `dolphin/types.h` as a thin compatibility/deprecated-macro wrapper. Added documentation comments for the platform types, introduced `enum_t` as an `int`-backed placeholder for not-yet-declared enums, introduced `any_t` as a `void*` placeholder distinct from `unk_t`, and adjusted several enum-like action-state locals/prototypes from `s32` to `enum_t`. The PR also changed `.clang-format` so empty functions may be formatted on one line, then applied that formatting broadly to empty stubs across dolphin, fighter, stage, game-mode, and baselib files.
what_changed: `include/Runtime/platform.h` gained the core integer, volatile integer, float, boolean, pointer, callback, jump-table, NULL/TRUE/FALSE, and `FLT_MAX` definitions that had previously lived in `include/dolphin/types.h`.

`include/dolphin/types.h` now includes `<Runtime/platform.h>` and retains only deprecated/legacy macro material such as `assert_line`, with a file-level TODO saying it can be removed when its macros are dealt with.
Added doc comments for many fundamental typedefs in `platform.h`, including `s8/u8`, volatile variants, `f32/f64`, `BOOL`, `unk_t`, `jmp_t`, `jtbl_t`, and `Event`.
Introduced `enum_t` as `typedef int enum_t;` for fields or locals known to represent an enum before the actual enum type has been created.
Introduced `any_t` as `typedef void* any_t;` for polymorphic or unspecified pointer data, with the PR body explicitly distinguishing it from `unk_t`.
Changed several enum-like action-state variables from `s32` to `enum_t`, including examples in Fox and Game & Watch special-move code.
Changed `func_800DEAE8` parameters in `src/melee/ft/ft_unknown_006.h` from `s32 asid0, s32 asid1` to `enum_t asid0, enum_t asid1`.
Applied the new empty-function formatting across many empty stubs, replacing multi-line empty bodies such as `void f() { }` or `return;`-only empty stubs with single-line `void f() {}` forms.
smart_moves: Centralized platform-wide primitive typedefs in `Runtime/platform.h`, making them available independently of the Dolphin SDK compatibility header and reducing the long-term dependency on `dolphin/types.h`.
Kept `dolphin/types.h` as a compatibility include rather than deleting it immediately, lowering churn and avoiding broad include breakage while documenting that it can eventually be removed.
Used `enum_t` instead of continuing to overload `s32` for enum placeholders; the PR body notes this is more searchable and platform-independent.
Called out a matching/codegen reason for `enum_t`: the author noted that `int` codegen can differ from `s32`, with `lblanguage` mentioned as a case where it matters.
Separated `any_t` from `unk_t` to communicate intent: `unk_t` means unknown type, while `any_t` means deliberately unspecified or polymorphic data such as HSD `user_data` or allocation results.
Made the formatting rule explicit in `.clang-format` before applying the tree-wide empty-function cleanup, so future formatting is reproducible rather than ad hoc.
decomp_lessons: Use `enum_t` for values known to be enums when the actual enum has not yet been declared; it preserves intent, is easy to grep, and may better match codegen than `s32` in some contexts.
Do not treat `s32` and `int` as always interchangeable in matching work; this PR explicitly warns that their codegen can differ, citing `lblanguage` and likely `pl` cases.
When migrating foundational typedefs, prefer a central platform header and leave old SDK-style headers as temporary wrappers until remaining macros or legacy declarations are relocated.

Document placeholder typedefs with intended migration paths. Here, `any_t` can later be found/replaced with `void*` once `unk_t` has been eliminated, while `enum_t` marks future enum extraction sites.
Formatting-only changes can be made less noisy long-term by encoding the rule in `.clang-format`; this PR’s empty-function cleanup should discourage hand-maintained multi-line empty stubs.
naming_conventions: `enum_t` means an `int`-backed placeholder for a real enum that has not been declared yet.
`any_t` means a `void*` used for intentionally polymorphic or unspecified data, distinct from `unk_t`.
`unk_t` remains a pointer to an unknown type and should not be used when the pointer is merely generic/polymorphic.
`BOOL` remains `typedef int BOOL`, with a TODO in `platform.h` to eventually rename or bridge it to native C++ `bool` under preprocessor conditions.
Action-state variables named `asid` are good candidates for `enum_t` while action-state enums are still incomplete.
Legacy `assert_line` is documented as really being `HSD_ASSERT` and belonging in `baselib/debug.h`, not permanently in `dolphin/types.h`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Prefer `enum_t`/`int` over `s32` for enum stand-ins when matching indicates signed-long versus int codegen differences; the PR author explicitly says `int` (`enum_t`) codegen differs from `s32`.
`lblanguage` is identified by the author as an example where the `int` versus `s32` distinction matters; similar issues may exist in `pl`.

Empty function bodies can be safely represented as one-line `{}` stubs under the updated clang-format rule; this is formatting rather than a logic/matching tactic except where an explicit empty function is needed as a stub.

The diff includes an unused `static asm void unused(void) {}` under `MUST_MATCH` in `Gecko_ExceptionPPC.c`, preserving the existing note that it triggers a peephole-off bug while changing only formatting.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the provided slice.

One author follow-up comment clarified that `int`/`enum_t` can generate different code from `s32`, which is why replacing enum stand-ins matters for matching; `lblanguage` was given as an example and `pl` was suspected to have similar cases.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining uses of `s32` locals or parameters with TODO comments indicating enum placeholders, especially action-state IDs.
Audit `lblanguage` and `pl` for places where `s32` should be `enum_t`/`int` for matching-sensitive codegen.
Find remaining direct dependencies on `include/dolphin/types.h` that could include `Runtime/platform.h` instead.
Move or replace the remaining `assert_line` macro with the intended `HSD_ASSERT`/`baselib/debug.h` home.
Search for `unk_t` uses that are actually generic/polymorphic pointers and should become `any_t`.
Search for empty functions not formatted as single-line `{}` after the clang-format rule change.
